page: Microsoft Azure FX64ms_v2 is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family, virtualized via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, configured with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs via hyper-threading. The memory subsystem provides 1344.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 21.0 GB per core. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and INT8 operations.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core Geekbench and Passmark CPU evaluations, alongside strong multi-core stress-ng results. Memory bandwidth tests indicate top-tier capabilities for large-block read operations at 397.18 GB/sec and write operations at 172.33 GB/sec, though the Passmark memory score falls in the average category. Single-core performance varies, with Geekbench single-core scoring in the top tier while stress-ng single-core registers as average. In application-specific workloads, the instance achieves top-tier results in database operations and static web serving throughput, and strong performance in Redis SET operations and medium-model LLM inference.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX64ms_v2 offers high memory density and compute capabilities suited for resource-intensive enterprise workloads, though it lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage. This trade-off means users pay for high-capacity RAM and dedicated compute performance while managing storage costs separately.

The hardware profile and benchmark results position this server for memory-intensive database operations, large-scale data processing, and multi-threaded enterprise applications. It is also suitable for software compilation, text processing, and medium-scale language model inference where high memory capacity and multi-core execution are required.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores and features 1344.0 GB of RAM, translating to 21.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth, though single-core stress-ng performance and Passmark memory scores remain average. The instance lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage, and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration is engineered for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1344 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX64ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1344 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring sixty-four vCPUs, high-speed Intel Xeon processing, and top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.

1344.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core memory density
